codeReview.summary: Feature adds the requested tool modules and broad test coverage, but two core dynamic-tool behaviors are not correctly wired, so the platform tool lifecycle requirements are not fully met.

issues.description: PlatformToolsManager is instantiated but never wired to runtime events (no addServer/registerIntegrationTools/deregisterIntegrationTools calls), so integration tool registration is not dynamically triggered on integration load/unload as required. Evidence: `_platformToolsManager` is unused and `registerIntegrationTools` / `deregisterIntegrationTools` are referenced only in tests.

`removeTool(name)` only deletes from `dynamicTools` map and does not remove already-registered tool handles from active sessions. This leaves stale tools callable in existing sessions after supposed deregistration, breaking dynamic deregister behavior.
`activeSessions` is hardcoded to 0 even though `McpHub` exposes `getSessionCount()`. This returns incorrect health data in `service__service_status`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature metadata uses `skillName: worker`, but the skill is unavailable in this environment; the worker transcript shows a direct deviation because the skill could not be invoked.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `features.json` for f4 has `skillName: worker`; transcript skeleton includes `Skill "worker" not found`; project `.factory/skills/` is empty.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Reviewer prompt expects architecture reference material under `.factory/library/architecture.md`, but this mission context only has `user-testing.md`, reducing shared architectural guidance for reviewers/workers.

summary: I reviewed the feature handoff, full commit diff (b2df875), and transcript skeleton. While coverage and tests are strong, dynamic platform-tool lifecycle behavior is not fully implemented in runtime wiring and dynamic tool removal semantics, so this review fails with actionable blocking issues.
